title: Lessingianthus regis
taxonomy: Lessingianthus regis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. It is further categorized as a member of the genus Lessingianthus, a genus of flowering plants native to the Americas. The plant is native to Brazil, where it is found growing in a variety of habitats. Lessingianthus regis is a relatively small plant, typically growing to a height of around 1-2 meters. Its classification reflects its botanical characteristics, including its distinctive flowers and foliage.

intro: Lessingianthus regis, also known as "Regis' Lessingianthus," is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and improve overall cardiovascular health. Bioactive constituents of Lessingianthus regis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lessingianthus regis include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation in the body.
